title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston experienced the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents in 2025, continuing a post-pandemic trend.
Everett and Worcester saw the highest population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively, as reported by the U.S. Census.
Housing costs and reduced immigration are cited as key factors for population shifts, with state's growth slowing to 0.2% last year.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments ranked 14th on Forbes' list with $37.7 billion annual revenue.
Demoulas Super Markets, Market Basket's parent company, ranked 69th with $8 billion revenue.
Forbes' list includes firms where founding families retain significant ownership and leadership.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major campus housing overhaul to modernize and expand facilities.
The housing project will occur in phases, allowing students to remain on campus during renovations or replacements of old buildings.
The plan aims to maintain affordability, enhance amenities, and align with sustainability goals amid growing enrollment and housing constraints.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre, Medford, showcasing songs from her album 'World’s Gone Wrong' with protest themes.
Williams included both new material and covers, such as Bob Marley's 'So Much Trouble in the World' and Neil Young's 'Rockin' in the Free World.'
Unable to play guitar post-stroke, Williams engaged the audience with spirited performances and political commentary on current issues.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox lost 8-6 to the Twins after Justin Slaten allowed four runs in one inning at Fenway Park.
Starting pitcher Payton Tolle had nine strikeouts but was pulled after six innings and 85 pitches by Chad Tracy.
Boston's offense, scoring six runs, was overshadowed by the Twins' larger offensive performance on Friday night.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw out the first pitch at Fenway Park before the Red Sox game against the Minnesota Twins.
Clemens, 63, tossed the pitch to his son, Twins player Kody Clemens, while wearing a custom "Rocket Man" jersey.
The event coincided with a giveaway of Roger Clemens bobbleheads commemorating his record-setting 20-strikeout games.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to NBA All-Defensive First Team for 2025-26 season with 58 votes.
White recorded career-highs of 98 blocks and 88 steals, placing him among top defenders in the league.
Celtics' defense improved significantly with White, being 7.4 points better per 100 possessions.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Tatiana Maslany stars in 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed,' now available on Apple TV.
'Glory' (1989), an Oscar-winning Civil War drama, is streaming on Netflix until June 1.
'Strangers with Candy,' featuring Stephen Colbert, is available on Paramount+.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet will begin throwing this weekend and has a live batting practice scheduled next week for recovery.
Roman Anthony remains sidelined due to pain tolerance issues after swinging a bat; no reinjury was reported.
Trevor Story will miss 6-10 weeks following sports hernia surgery, impacting the Red Sox lineup.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox president Sam Kennedy confirms early discussions to add a hitter amid an underperforming 2026 season.
Injuries and poor performances have hampered Boston's offense, setting them two games out of a wild-card spot.
Red Sox rank 3rd worst in MLB slugging percentage and seek to boost lineup before Aug. 3 trade deadline.
